items.text: liminal-sdk crate exists at crates/liminal-sdk/ with Cargo.toml declaring dependencies on liminal core and protocol crates.
liminal-gleam package exists at sdks/liminal-gleam/ with gleam.toml declaring FFI dependency on liminal-sdk.
liminal-ts package exists at sdks/liminal-ts/ with package.json and TypeScript build configuration.
ChannelHandle defined in liminal_sdk::channel with publish, subscribe, and request_reply methods.
ConversationHandle defined in liminal_sdk::conversation with send, receive, and lifecycle observation methods.
SchemaValidate trait defined in liminal_sdk::types for compile-time type enforcement on publish and subscribe.
Backpressure response enum (Accept, Defer, Reject) defined in liminal_sdk::pressure and returned from all publish operations.
Embedded mode provides in-process channel handles without serialisation or network overhead.
Remote mode connects over the wire protocol with the protocol implementation fully internal to the SDK.
Connection lifecycle state machine (connecting, connected, reconnecting, disconnected) implemented with observable state transitions.
Reconnection uses exponential backoff with jitter, configurable base delay and max delay.
Subscription state recovery replays from the client's last acknowledged sequence number on reconnect.
Connection pool supports multiple concurrent channel subscriptions over configurable connection count.
Gleam channel module provides typed publish and subscribe functions with compile-time schema enforcement.
Conversation handler behaviour (init, handle_message, handle_timeout, terminate) defined as a Gleam behaviour.
FFI bindings delegate transport operations to the Rust SDK without re-implementing protocol logic.
Channel<T> and Conversation<T> generic interfaces defined with runtime type validation via JSON Schema.
WASM bridge compiles protocol layer from Rust and exposes it to browser TypeScript.
Type generator produces TypeScript interfaces from liminal channel type definitions.
Behavioural conformance tests for connection lifecycle, subscription recovery, and backpressure handling pass against all three SDKs.
